name: Naadam Festival
traditions: Three manly games: horse racing (children jockeys), wrestling, and archery; ankle-bone shooting; traditional music; airag (fermented mare's milk).
historicalBackground: Naadam originated from military exercises of Genghis Khan's warriors. It has been celebrated for centuries as a test of strength and skill on the Mongolian steppe.
description: Mongolia's ancient Festival of the Three Manly Games, featuring horse racing, wrestling, and archery in a celebration of nomadic warrior culture.
